𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Nineteen — Part Six

The Book of Yehezkel

YEHEZKEL

The Book of Ezekiel — 4QEzeka · 4QEzekb · 11QEzek Primary

Chapters 40 through 48

The New Temple Vision — The Kavod Returns — The River From the Temple — The Division of the Land — YHWH Is There

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QEzeka · 4QEzekb · 11QEzek — Dead Sea Scrolls — Cave 4 and Cave 11 Qumran

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTERS 40 THROUGH 48 — THE NEW TEMPLE VISION AND THE RESTORED COVENANT LAND

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 40 The New Temple Vision — The Man With the Measuring Reed — The Outer Court — The Gates — The Inner Court

1 In the twenty-fifth year of our exile, at the beginning of the year, on the tenth day of the month, in the fourteenth year after the city was struck down, on that very day, the hand of YHWH was upon me, and he brought me to the city.

2 In visions of YHWH he brought me to the land of Yisra'EL, and set me down on a very high mountain, on which was a structure like a city to the south.

3 When he brought me there, behold, there was a man whose appearance was like bronze, with a linen cord and a measuring reed in his hand. And he was standing in the gateway.

4 And the man said to me, Son of man, look with your eyes, and hear with your ears, and set your heart upon all that I shall show you, for you were brought here in order that I might show it to you. Declare all that you see to the house of Yisra'EL.

5 And behold, there was a wall all around the outside of the temple area, and the length of the measuring reed in the man's hand was six long cubits, each being a cubit and a handbreadth in length. So he measured the thickness of the wall, one reed, and the height, one reed.

6 Then he went into the gateway facing east, going up its steps, and measured the threshold of the gate, one reed deep.

7 And the side rooms, one reed long and one reed wide, and the space between the side rooms, five cubits. And the threshold of the gate by the vestibule of the gate at the inner end, one reed.

8 Then he measured the vestibule of the gateway, on the inside, one reed.

9 Then he measured the vestibule of the gate, eight cubits, and its jambs, two cubits. And the vestibule of the gate was on the inside.

10 And the side rooms of the gate on the east side were three on each side. The three were of the same size, and the jambs on either side were of the same size.

11 Then he measured the width of the opening of the gateway, ten cubits, and the length of the gate, thirteen cubits.

12 There was a barrier before the side rooms, one cubit on either side. And the side rooms were six cubits on either side.

13 Then he measured the gate from the ceiling of one side room to the ceiling of the other, a width of twenty-five cubits, door to door.

14 He made the jambs sixty cubits high, and the court extended to the jambs on every side.

15 From the front of the gate at the entrance to the front of the inner vestibule of the gate was fifty cubits.

16 And the gateway had recessed windows all around, and likewise the vestibule had windows all around inside, and on the jambs were palm trees.

17 Then he brought me into the outer court. And behold, there were chambers and a pavement, all around the court. Thirty chambers faced the pavement.

18 The pavement ran alongside the gates, corresponding to the length of the gates. This was the lower pavement.

19 Then he measured the distance from the inner front of the lower gate to the outer front of the inner court, a hundred cubits on the east and on the north.

20 As for the gate that faced toward the north, belonging to the outer court, he measured its length and its width.

21 Its side rooms, three on either side, and its jambs and its vestibule were of the same size as those of the first gate. Its length was fifty cubits, and its width twenty-five cubits.

22 And its windows, its vestibule, and its palm trees were of the same size as those of the gate that faced toward the east. And it was approached by seven steps, and its vestibule was on the inside.

23 And opposite the gate on the north, as on the east, was a gate to the inner court. And he measured from gate to gate, a hundred cubits.

24 And he led me toward the south, and behold, there was a gate on the south. And he measured its jambs and its vestibule. They had the same dimensions as the others.

25 And there were windows all around in it and in its vestibule, like the windows of the others. Its length was fifty cubits, and its width twenty-five cubits.

26 And there were seven steps leading up to it, and its vestibule was on the inside, and it had palm trees on its jambs, one on either side.

27 And there was a gate to the inner court toward the south. And he measured from gate to gate toward the south, a hundred cubits.

28 Then he brought me to the inner court through the south gate, and he measured the south gate. It was the same size as the others.

29 Its side rooms, its jambs, and its vestibule were of the same size as the others. And there were windows all around in it and in its vestibule. Its length was fifty cubits, and its width twenty-five cubits.

30 And there were vestibules all around, twenty-five cubits long and five cubits wide.

31 Its vestibule faced the outer court, and palm trees were on its jambs, and its stairway had eight steps.

32 Then he brought me to the inner court on the east side, and he measured the gate. It was the same size as the others.

33 Its side rooms, its jambs, and its vestibule were of the same size as the others. And there were windows all around in it and in its vestibule. Its length was fifty cubits, and its width twenty-five cubits.

34 Its vestibule faced the outer court, and it had palm trees on its jambs on either side, and its stairway had eight steps.

35 Then he brought me to the north gate, and he measured it. It had the same dimensions as the others.

36 Its side rooms, its jambs, and its vestibule were of the same size. And there were windows all around it. Its length was fifty cubits, and its width twenty-five cubits.

37 Its vestibule faced the outer court, and it had palm trees on its jambs on either side, and its stairway had eight steps.

38 There was a chamber with its door in the vestibule of the gate, where the burnt offering was to be washed.

39 And in the vestibule of the gate were two tables on either side, on which the burnt offering and the sin offering and the guilt offering were to be slaughtered.

40 And on the outside of the vestibule at the entrance of the north gate were two tables, and on the other side of the vestibule of the gate were two tables.

41 Four tables were on either side of the gate, eight tables, on which to slaughter.

42 And there were four tables of hewn stone for the burnt offering, a cubit and a half long, and a cubit and a half wide, and one cubit high, on which the instruments were to be laid with which the burnt offerings and the sacrifices were slaughtered.

43 And hooks, a handbreadth long, were fastened all around the inside. And on the tables the flesh of the offering was to be laid.

44 On the outside of the inner gateway there were two chambers in the inner court, one at the side of the north gate facing south, the other at the side of the south gate facing north.

45 And he said to me, This chamber that faces south is for the priests who have charge of the temple,

46 And the chamber that faces north is for the priests who have charge of the altar. These are the sons of Tzadok, who alone among the sons of Levi may come near to YHWH to minister to him.

47 And he measured the court, a hundred cubits long and a hundred cubits wide, a square. And the altar was in front of the temple.

48 Then he brought me to the vestibule of the temple and measured the jambs of the vestibule, five cubits on either side. And the width of the gate was fourteen cubits, and the sidewalls of the gate were three cubits on either side.

49 The length of the vestibule was twenty cubits, and the width twelve cubits, and it was reached by steps. And there were pillars beside the jambs, one on either side.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 11QEzek and 4QEzekb preserve portions of the temple vision. The new temple vision of Chapters 40-48 is the covenant architectural revelation: YHWH shows Yehezkel precise measurements of the covenant restoration sanctuary. Dated to the twenty-fifth year of exile — the jubilee year — the covenant year of release and restoration. The covenant architectural precision is its own covenant message: YHWH's plans are detailed, measured, and specific.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 40 in full. Verse 46 — the sons of Tzadok, who alone among the sons of Levi may come near to YHWH to minister to him. The Tzadokite priesthood is elevated as the covenant legitimate priestly line — those who remained faithful when the rest of Yisra'EL went astray. The covenant architectural vision is simultaneously a covenant priestly constitution.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 40. The measuring reed of six long cubits — verse 5 — is the covenant standard throughout the temple vision. Every measurement is exact. The covenant sanctuary is not an impressionistic vision but a precisely dimensioned covenant structure. The covenant precision communicates covenant reality: this is an actual covenant structure, not a metaphor.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 4 — look with your eyes, and hear with your ears, and set your heart upon all that I shall show you, for you were brought here in order that I might show it to you. Declare all that you see to the house of Yisra'EL. The covenant commission of the temple vision is pedagogical: Yehezkel is brought to see in order to declare. The exiles in Babylon receive through Yehezkel's report a precise covenant description of what YHWH has planned. The covenant word creates covenant reality before the covenant stones are laid.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 41 The Nave and the Inner Room — The Cherubim and Palm Trees — The Dimensions of the Temple — The Wooden Altar

1 Then he brought me to the nave and measured the jambs. On each side the jambs were six cubits wide, which was the width of the tent.

2 And the width of the entrance was ten cubits, and the sidewalls of the entrance were five cubits on either side. And he measured the length of the nave, forty cubits, and its width, twenty cubits.

3 Then he went into the inner room and measured the jambs of the entrance, two cubits, and the entrance, six cubits, and the sidewalls on either side of the entrance, seven cubits.

4 And he measured the length of the room, twenty cubits, and its width, twenty cubits, across the nave. And he said to me, This is the Most Holy Place.

5 Then he measured the wall of the temple, six cubits thick, and the width of the side chambers, four cubits, all around the temple.

6 The side chambers were in three stories, one over another, thirty in each story. There were offsets in the wall of the temple all around to serve as supports for the side chambers, so that they should not be supported by the wall of the temple.

7 And the side chambers became broader as they went higher, corresponding to the widening of the offset from story to story all around the temple. On the higher levels the width increased. Thus one went up from the lowest story to the highest by way of the middle story.

8 I saw also that the temple had a raised platform all around. The foundations of the side chambers measured a full reed of six long cubits.

9 The thickness of the outer wall of the side chambers was five cubits. The free space between the side chambers of the temple and

10 The chambers of the court was a width of twenty cubits all around the temple on every side.

11 And the doors of the side chambers opened on the free space, one door toward the north, and another door toward the south. And the width of the free space was five cubits all around.

12 The building that was facing the separate yard on the west side was seventy cubits wide, and the wall of the building was five cubits thick all around, and its depth ninety cubits.

13 Then he measured the temple, a hundred cubits long. And the yard and the building with its walls were a hundred cubits long.

14 The width of the front of the temple and the yard on the east was a hundred cubits.

15 Then he measured the depth of the building facing the yard that was at the west and its galleries on either side, a hundred cubits.

16 The nave, the inner room, and the outer vestibule were paneled and around all three had windows with recessed frames. Over against the threshold the temple was paneled with wood all around, from the floor up to the windows — and the windows were covered —

17 To the space above the door, even to the inner room, and on the outside. And on all the walls all around, inside and outside, was a measured pattern.

18 It was carved with cherubim and palm trees, a palm tree between cherub and cherub. Every cherub had two faces:

19 A human face toward the palm tree on the one side, and the face of a young lion toward the palm tree on the other side. They were carved throughout all the temple all around.

20 From the floor to above the door, cherubim and palm trees were carved, on the wall of the nave.

21 The nave had a square doorframe, and in front of the Most Holy Place was something resembling

22 An altar of wood, three cubits high, two cubits long, and two cubits wide. Its corners, its base, and its sides were of wood. He said to me, This is the table that is before YHWH.

23 The nave and the Most Holy Place had each a double door.

24 The double doors had two leaves apiece, two swinging leaves for each door.

25 And on the doors of the nave were carved cherubim and palm trees, such as were carved on the walls. And there was a canopy of wood in front of the vestibule on the outside.

26 And there were recessed windows and palm trees on either side, on the sidewalls of the vestibule, the side chambers of the temple, and the canopies.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 41 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The interior of the covenant temple — nave, inner room, Most Holy Place — is described with the same covenant precision as the outer courts. The cherubim and palm tree motif carved throughout — verse 18-20 — is the covenant artistic program: covenant guardian beings flanking covenant trees of life, echoing the original covenant garden of Eden.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 41 in full. Verse 22 — this is the table that is before YHWH. The covenant renaming of the incense altar as the table: in the restoration temple, the altar is called the table before YHWH. The covenant meal imagery: YHWH's covenant presence is mediated through the table set before him.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 41. The cherubim with two faces — verse 18-19 — human face and lion face — contrasts with the four-faced cherubim of the Merkavah vision. The covenant architectural cherubim are the same covenant beings in a different covenant register appropriate to carved sanctuary decoration.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The architectural precision of Chapter 41 has generated centuries of scholarly reconstruction. The covenant importance of the precision is not the production of blueprints but the covenant communication of specificity: YHWH's covenant plans are not vague or impressionistic. The covenant future is as precisely real as the covenant past measured in Shemot 25-27.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 42 The Chambers for the Priests — A Holy Place Where the Priests Eat the Most Holy Things — The Outer Wall Measured

1 Then he led me out into the outer court, toward the north, and he brought me to the chambers that were opposite the separate yard and opposite the building on the north.

2 The length of the building whose door faced north was a hundred cubits, and the width fifty cubits.

3 Facing the twenty cubits that belonged to the inner court, and facing the pavement that belonged to the outer court, was gallery against gallery in three stories.

4 And before the chambers was a passage inward, ten cubits wide and a hundred cubits long, and their doors were on the north.

5 Now the upper chambers were narrower, for the galleries took more away from them than from the lower and middle chambers of the building.

6 For they were in three stories, and they had no pillars like the pillars of the outer court. Thus the upper chambers were set back from the ground more than the lower and the middle ones.

7 And the wall that was outside parallel to the chambers, toward the outer court, opposite the chambers, was fifty cubits long.

8 For the chambers of the outer court were fifty cubits long, while those facing the temple were a hundred cubits long.

9 Below these chambers was an entrance on the east side, as one enters them from the outer court.

10 In the thickness of the wall of the court, toward the east, opposite the separate yard and opposite the building, there were chambers.

11 The passage before them was similar to the passages of the chambers on the north, of the same length and width, with the same exits and arrangements and the same doors.

12 And below the south chambers was an entrance on the east side, as one enters, with a pillar on either side of the passage.

13 Then he said to me, The north chambers and the south chambers opposite the separate yard are the holy chambers, where the priests who approach YHWH shall eat the most holy offerings. There they shall put the most holy offerings — the grain offering, the sin offering, and the guilt offering — for the place is holy.

14 When the priests enter, they shall not go out of the holy place into the outer court without laying there the garments in which they minister, for these are holy. They shall put on other garments before they go near to that which is for the people.

15 Now when he had finished measuring the interior of the temple area, he led me out by the gate that faced east, and measured the temple area all around.

16 He measured the east side with the measuring reed, five hundred cubits by the measuring reed all around.

17 He measured the north side, five hundred cubits by the measuring reed all around.

18 He measured the south side, five hundred cubits by the measuring reed.

19 Then he turned to the west side and measured, five hundred cubits by the measuring reed.

20 He measured it on the four sides. It had a wall around it, five hundred cubits long and five hundred cubits wide, to make a separation between the holy and the common.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 42 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The priestly chambers — where the priests eat the most holy offerings — are the covenant transition zones between the holy and the common. Verse 20 — the wall makes a separation between the holy and the common. The covenant architectural principle of havdalah is built into the physical structure: the wall itself is the covenant distinction.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 42 in full. Verse 14 — when priests enter the holy place they shall not go out without laying aside their holy garments. The covenant priestly protocol of transition: garments worn in the Most Holy Place cannot be worn near the people without covenant contamination. The covenant hierarchy of holiness requires covenant protocols of transition.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 42. The outer wall measuring 500 cubits on each side establishes the covenant perimeter of the entire sacred precinct. The covenant sanctuary is bounded by a wall whose purpose is explicitly stated: to make a separation between the holy and the common. The covenant architecture embodies the covenant theology of distinction.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13 — the holy chambers, where the priests who approach YHWH shall eat the most holy offerings. The covenant priestly meal within the sanctuary precinct: the priests consume the covenant offerings in a designated covenant space. The covenant consumption of the most holy things is itself a covenant act — the priest's participation in the offering by eating it is integral to the covenant sacrificial system.

THE KAVOD RETURNS — THE COVENANT CONSTITUTION — CHAPTERS 43 THROUGH 48

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 43 The Kavod Returns to the Temple — The Voice of Many Waters — The Place of My Throne — Son of Man Describe the Temple — The Altar and Its Measurements

1 Then he led me to the gate, the gate facing east.

2 And behold, the kavod of the Creator of Yisra'EL was coming from the east. And the sound of his coming was like the sound of many waters, and the earth shone with his kavod.

3 And the vision I saw was just like the vision that I had seen when he came to destroy the city, and just like the vision that I had seen by the Kevar river. And I fell on my face.

4 As the kavod of YHWH entered the temple by the gate facing east,

5 The Spirit lifted me up and brought me into the inner court, and behold, the kavod of YHWH filled the temple.

6 While the man was standing beside me, I heard one speaking to me out of the temple,

7 And he said to me, Son of man, this is the place of my throne and the place of the soles of my feet, where I will dwell in the midst of the people of Yisra'EL forever. And the house of Yisra'EL shall no more defile my holy name, neither they nor their kings, by their whoring and by the dead bodies of their kings at their high places.

8 When they placed their threshold by my threshold and their doorposts beside my doorposts, with only a wall between me and them, they would defile my holy name by their abominations that they committed. So I have consumed them in my anger.

9 Now let them put away their whoring and the dead bodies of their kings far from me, and I will dwell in their midst forever.

10 As for you, son of man, describe to the house of Yisra'EL the temple, that they may be ashamed of their iniquities, and let them measure the plan.

11 And if they are ashamed of all that they have done, make known to them the design of the temple, its arrangement, its exits and its entrances, its whole design and all its statutes, its whole design and all its laws. And write it down in their sight, so that they may observe all its laws and all its statutes and carry them out.

12 This is the law of the temple: the whole territory on the top of the mountain all around shall be most holy. Behold, this is the law of the temple.

13 These are the measurements of the altar by cubits — the cubit being a cubit and a handbreadth. Its base shall be one cubit high and one cubit wide, with a rim of one span around its edge. And this shall be the height of the altar.

14 From the base on the ground to the lower ledge, two cubits, with a width of one cubit. And from the smaller ledge to the larger ledge, four cubits, with a width of one cubit.

15 The altar hearth was four cubits high, and from the altar hearth there were four horns projecting upward.

16 The altar hearth was twelve cubits long by twelve cubits wide, a square.

17 The ledge also was fourteen cubits long by fourteen cubits wide on its four sides, with a rim of half a cubit around it, and its base one cubit around. And its steps faced east.

18 And he said to me, Son of man, thus says the Creator YHWH: These are the ordinances for the altar: On the day when it is erected for offering burnt offerings upon it and for sprinkling blood against it,

19 You shall give to the Levitical priests of the family of Tzadok, who draw near to me to minister to me, declares the Creator YHWH, a bull from the herd for a sin offering.

20 And you shall take some of its blood and put it on the four horns of the altar and on the four corners of the ledge and upon the rim all around. Thus you shall purify the altar and make atonement for it.

21 You shall also take the bull of the sin offering, and it shall be burned in the appointed place belonging to the temple, outside the sacred area.

22 And on the second day you shall offer a male goat without blemish for a sin offering. And the altar shall be purified, as it was purified with the bull.

23 When you have finished purifying it, you shall offer a bull from the herd without blemish and a ram from the flock without blemish.

24 You shall present them before YHWH, and the priests shall sprinkle salt on them and offer them up as a burnt offering to YHWH.

25 For seven days you shall provide daily a male goat for a sin offering. Also, a bull from the herd and a ram from the flock, without blemish, shall be provided.

26 Seven days shall they make atonement for the altar and cleanse it, and so consecrate it.

27 And when they have completed these days, then from the eighth day onward the priests shall offer on the altar your burnt offerings and your peace offerings, and I will accept you, declares the Creator YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 11QEzek and 4QEzekb preserve portions of Chapter 43. The return of the kavod to the new temple — verse 2-5 — is the covenant climax of the entire book: the same kavod that departed from the old temple in Chapters 10-11, arriving by the same eastern route, now returns permanently. The covenant trajectory of the entire book is kavod-departure, exile, restoration, kavod-return.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 43 in full. Verse 7 — this is the place of my throne and the place of the soles of my feet, where I will dwell in the midst of the people of Yisra'EL forever. The covenant permanence of the new dwelling: the kavod that returned will not depart again. The condition for permanent divine indwelling — verse 9 — is the permanent removal of covenant whoring.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 43. Verse 2 — the sound of his coming was like the sound of many waters. The covenant sonic marker connecting the kavod's return to the Merkavah vision of Chapter 1:24. The covenant architectural vision of Chapters 40-42 is now filled with the covenant presence it was designed to house.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 10-11 — describe to the house of Yisra'EL the temple, that they may be ashamed of their iniquities. The covenant purpose of describing the new temple to the exiles is not merely informational but transformational: the vision of what YHWH is planning is intended to produce covenant shame for what they have done. The covenant future serves as the covenant mirror in which the covenant past is finally seen clearly.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 44 The Shut Eastern Gate — No Foreigner Uncircumcised in Heart — The Levites Who Went Far From Me — The Sons of Tzadok — Their Inheritance Is YHWH

1 Then he brought me back to the outer gate of the sanctuary, which faces east. And it was shut.

2 And YHWH said to me, This gate shall remain shut. It shall not be opened, and no one shall enter by it, for YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, has entered by it. Therefore it shall remain shut.

3 Only the prince may sit in it to eat bread before YHWH. He shall enter by way of the vestibule of the gate, and shall go out by the same way.

4 Then he brought me by way of the north gate to the front of the temple, and I looked, and behold, the kavod of YHWH filled the temple of YHWH. And I fell on my face.

5 And YHWH said to me, Son of man, mark well, see with your eyes, and hear with your ears all that I shall tell you concerning all the statutes of the temple of YHWH and all its laws. And mark well the entrance to the temple and all the exits from the sanctuary.

6 And say to the rebellious house, to the house of Yisra'EL, Thus says the Creator YHWH: O house of Yisra'EL, enough of all your abominations,

7 In admitting foreigners, uncircumcised in heart and flesh, to be in my sanctuary, profaning my temple, when you offer to me my food, the fat and the blood. You have broken my covenant, in addition to all your abominations.

8 And you have not kept charge of my holy things, but you have set others to keep my charge for you in my sanctuary.

9 Thus says the Creator YHWH: No foreigner, uncircumcised in heart and flesh, of all the foreigners who are among the people of Yisra'EL, shall enter my sanctuary.

10 But the Levites who went far from me, going astray from me after their idols when Yisra'EL went astray, shall bear their punishment.

11 They shall be ministers in my sanctuary, having oversight at the gates of the temple and ministering in the temple. They shall slaughter the burnt offering and the sacrifice for the people, and they shall stand before the people, to minister to them.

12 Because they ministered to them before their idols and became a stumbling block of iniquity to the house of Yisra'EL, therefore I have sworn concerning them, declares the Creator YHWH, and they shall bear their punishment.

13 They shall not come near to me, to serve me as priest, nor come near any of my holy things and the things that are most holy, but they shall bear their shame and the abominations that they have committed.

14 Yet I will appoint them to keep charge of the temple, to do all its service and all that is to be done in it.

15 But the Levitical priests, the sons of Tzadok, who kept the charge of my sanctuary when the people of Yisra'EL went astray from me, shall come near to me to minister to me. And they shall stand before me to offer me the fat and the blood, declares the Creator YHWH.

16 They shall enter my sanctuary, and they shall approach my table, to minister to me, and they shall keep my charge.

17 When they enter the gates of the inner court, they shall wear linen garments. They shall have nothing of wool on them, while they minister at the gates of the inner court, and within.

18 They shall have linen turbans on their heads, and linen undergarments around their waists. They shall not bind themselves with anything that causes sweat.

19 And when they go out into the outer court to the people, they shall put off the garments in which they have been ministering and lay them in the holy chambers. And they shall put on other garments, lest they transmit holiness to the people with their garments.

20 They shall not shave their heads or let their locks grow long. They shall surely trim the hair of their heads.

21 No priest shall drink wine when he enters the inner court.

22 They shall not marry a widow or a divorced woman, but only virgins of the offspring of the house of Yisra'EL, or a widow who is the widow of a priest.

23 They shall teach my people the difference between the holy and the common, and show them how to distinguish between the unclean and the clean.

24 In a dispute, they shall act as judges, and they shall judge it according to my judgments. They shall keep my laws and my statutes in all my appointed feasts, and they shall keep my Shabbatot holy.

25 They shall not defile themselves by going near to a dead person. However, for father or mother, for son or daughter, for brother or unmarried sister they may defile themselves.

26 After he is defiled, he shall count seven days for his cleansing.

27 And on the day that he goes into the holy place, into the inner court, to minister in the holy place, he shall offer his sin offering, declares the Creator YHWH.

28 They shall have no inheritance. I am their inheritance. And you shall give them no possession in Yisra'EL. I am their possession.

29 They shall eat the grain offering, the sin offering, and the guilt offering, and every devoted thing in Yisra'EL shall be theirs.

30 And the first of all the firstfruits of all kinds, and every offering of all kinds from all your offerings, shall belong to the priests. You shall also give to the priests the first of your dough, that a blessing may rest on your house.

31 The priests shall not eat of anything, whether bird or beast, that died of itself or was torn by wild animals.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 44 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The permanently shut eastern gate — through which YHWH's kavod entered — is the covenant architectural declaration of the permanence of YHWH's return. The covenant gate that brought the kavod in shall not be opened for egress: YHWH is not departing again.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 44 in full. Verse 28 — they shall have no inheritance. I am their inheritance. The covenant economic provision for the Tzadokite priesthood: YHWH himself is their inheritance. No land allotment, no territorial portion — the covenant priests' portion is the covenant presence itself and the covenant offerings that flow from it.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 44. Verse 23 — they shall teach my people the difference between the holy and the common, and show them how to distinguish between the unclean and the clean. The covenant priestly teaching function restored: the covenant failure identified in Chapter 22:26 — priests who made no distinction between holy and common — is now the primary covenant assignment of the restored Tzadokite priesthood.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9 — no foreigner uncircumcised in heart and flesh shall enter my sanctuary. The covenant dual criterion of circumcision: both physical — flesh — and covenantal — heart. The covenant failure of the old temple administration — verse 7 — was admitting those physically uncircumcised. But the covenant standard requires both: covenant physical sign and covenant heart alignment. The covenant reform of the new temple addresses the covenant collapse of distinction that characterized the old.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 45 The Holy Portion of the Land — The Prince's Portion — Just Weights and Measures — The Passover — The Feast of Booths

1 When you allot the land as an inheritance, you shall set apart for YHWH a portion of the land as a holy district, twenty-five thousand cubits long and twenty thousand cubits wide. It shall be holy throughout its whole extent.

2 Of this, a square plot of five hundred by five hundred cubits shall be for the sanctuary, with fifty cubits around it for an open space.

3 And from this measured district you shall measure off a section twenty-five thousand cubits long and ten thousand cubits wide, in which shall be the sanctuary, the Most Holy Place.

4 It shall be the holy portion of the land. It shall be for the priests, who minister in the sanctuary and approach YHWH to minister to him, and it shall be a place for their houses and a holy place for the sanctuary.

5 Another section, twenty-five thousand cubits long and ten thousand cubits wide, shall be for the Levites who minister in the temple, as their possession for cities to live in.

6 Alongside the portion set apart as the holy district, you shall assign for the possession of the city an area five thousand cubits wide and twenty-five thousand cubits long. It shall belong to the whole house of Yisra'EL.

7 And to the prince shall belong the land on both sides of the holy district and the city's property. Extending from the western to the eastern boundary of the land, it shall border the tribal portions on the west and on the east.

8 It is to be his property in Yisra'EL. And my princes shall no more oppress my people, but they shall let the house of Yisra'EL have the land according to their tribes.

9 Thus says the Creator YHWH: Enough, O princes of Yisra'EL. Put away violence and oppression, and execute justice and righteousness. Cease your evictions of my people, declares the Creator YHWH.

10 You shall have just balances, a just ephah, and a just bath.

11 The ephah and the bath shall be of the same measure, the bath containing one tenth of a homer, and the ephah one tenth of a homer. The homer shall be the standard measure.

12 The shekel shall be twenty gerahs. Twenty shekels plus twenty-five shekels plus fifteen shekels shall be your mina.

13 This is the offering that you shall make: one sixth of an ephah from each homer of wheat, and one sixth of an ephah from each homer of barley,

14 And as the fixed portion of oil, one tenth of a bath from each cor, ten baths being a homer, for ten baths make a homer.

15 And one sheep from every flock of two hundred, from the watering places of Yisra'EL for grain offering, burnt offering, and peace offerings, to make atonement for them, declares the Creator YHWH.

16 All the people of the land shall be obligated to give this offering to the prince in Yisra'EL.

17 It shall be the prince's duty to furnish the burnt offerings, grain offerings, and drink offerings, at the feasts, the new moons, and the Shabbatot, all the appointed feasts of the house of Yisra'EL. He shall provide the sin offerings, grain offerings, burnt offerings, and peace offerings, to make atonement for the house of Yisra'EL.

18 Thus says the Creator YHWH: In the first month, on the first day of the month, you shall take a bull from the herd without blemish, and purify the sanctuary.

19 The priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering and put it on the doorposts of the temple, the four corners of the ledge of the altar, and the doorposts of the gate of the inner court.

20 You shall do the same on the seventh day of the month for anyone who has sinned through error or ignorance. So you shall make atonement for the temple.

21 In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, you shall celebrate the Feast of the Passover, and for seven days unleavened bread shall be eaten.

22 On that day the prince shall provide for himself and all the people of the land a young bull for a sin offering.

23 And on the seven days of the feast he shall provide as a burnt offering to YHWH seven young bulls and seven rams without blemish, on each of the seven days, and a male goat daily for a sin offering.

24 And he shall provide as a grain offering an ephah for each bull, an ephah for each ram, and a hin of oil to each ephah.

25 In the seventh month, on the fifteenth day of the month and for the seven days of the feast, he shall make the same provision for sin offerings, burnt offerings, and grain offerings, and for the oil.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 45 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The land allotment for the covenant sanctuary precinct — the holy district of 25,000 by 20,000 cubits — is the covenant geographic center of the restored land. The covenant measurements are arranged concentrically: sanctuary at center, priestly portion surrounding it, Levitical portion adjacent, city portion, and prince's portions on either side.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 45 in full. Verse 9 — Enough, O princes of Yisra'EL. Put away violence and oppression, and execute justice and righteousness. The covenant political constitution of the restoration: the prince's covenant obligation is not covenant privilege but covenant justice. The covenant accusation against the pre-exilic princes — violence and oppression — becomes the covenant prohibition against the restoration prince.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 45. Verse 10-12 — just balances, just ephah, just bath, standardized shekel. The covenant economic constitution: the restoration covenant community operates with covenant-calibrated measures. The covenant commercial fraud that was endemic in the pre-exilic community — Amos 8:5, Mikah 6:11 — is addressed by covenant architectural embedding of just measures in the covenant constitution.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 8 — my princes shall no more oppress my people, but they shall let the house of Yisra'EL have the land according to their tribes. The covenant land reform embedded in the covenant constitution: the pre-exilic royal land grab — Melakhim Aleph 21, the Navot incident — is reversed. The prince has a defined portion; the tribes have their portions; the covenant boundary between them is the covenant protection of every family's covenant inheritance.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 46 The Prince at the East Gate — Shabbat and New Moon Offerings — The Prince Shall Not Take From the People's Inheritance — The Kitchens of the Temple

1 Thus says the Creator YHWH: The gate of the inner court that faces east shall be shut on the six working days, but on the Shabbat day it shall be opened, and on the day of the new moon it shall be opened.

2 The prince shall enter by the vestibule of the gate from outside, and shall take his stand by the post of the gate. The priests shall offer his burnt offering and his peace offerings, and he shall bow down at the threshold of the gate. Then he shall go out, but the gate shall not be shut until evening.

3 The people of the land shall bow down at the entrance of that gate before YHWH on the Shabbatot and on the new moons.

4 The burnt offering that the prince offers to YHWH on the Shabbat day shall be six lambs without blemish and a ram without blemish.

5 And the grain offering with the ram shall be an ephah, and the grain offering with the lambs shall be as much as he is able to give, together with a hin of oil to each ephah.

6 On the day of the new moon he shall offer a young bull without blemish, and six lambs and a ram, which shall be without blemish.

7 As a grain offering he shall provide an ephah with the bull and an ephah with the ram, and with the lambs as much as he is able, together with a hin of oil to each ephah.

8 When the prince enters, he shall enter by the vestibule of the gate, and he shall go out by the same way.

9 When the people of the land come before YHWH at the appointed feasts, he who enters by the north gate to worship shall go out by the south gate, and he who enters by the south gate shall go out by the north gate. No one shall return by way of the gate by which he entered, but each shall go out straight ahead.

10 When they enter, the prince shall enter with them, and when they go out, he shall go out.

11 At the feasts and the appointed festivals, the grain offering with a young bull shall be an ephah, and with a ram an ephah, and with the lambs as much as one is able to give, and a hin of oil to an ephah.

12 When the prince provides a freewill offering, either a burnt offering or peace offerings as a freewill offering to YHWH, the gate facing east shall be opened for him. And he shall offer his burnt offering or his peace offerings as he does on the Shabbat day. Then he shall go out, and after he has gone out the gate shall be shut.

13 You shall provide a lamb a year old without blemish as a burnt offering to YHWH daily. Morning by morning you shall provide it.

14 And you shall provide a grain offering with it morning by morning, one sixth of an ephah, and one third of a hin of oil to moisten the flour, as a grain offering to YHWH. This is a perpetual statute.

15 Thus the lamb and the meal offering and the oil shall be provided, morning by morning, as a regular burnt offering.

16 Thus says the Creator YHWH: If the prince makes a gift to any of his sons as his inheritance, it shall belong to his sons. It is their property by inheritance.

17 But if he makes a gift out of his inheritance to one of his servants, it shall be his until the year of liberty. Then it shall revert to the prince. His inheritance shall be for his sons only.

18 The prince shall not take any of the inheritance of the people, thrusting them out of their property. He shall give his sons their inheritance out of his own property, so that none of my people shall be scattered from his property.

19 Then he brought me through the entrance, which was at the side of the gate, to the north row of the holy chambers for the priests, and behold, there was a place at the far western end of them.

20 And he said to me, This is the place where the priests shall boil the guilt offering and the sin offering, and where they shall bake the grain offering, in order not to bring them out into the outer court and so transmit holiness to the people.

21 Then he brought me out to the outer court and led me around to the four corners of the court, and behold, in each corner of the court there was another court.

22 In the four corners of the court were small courts, forty cubits long and thirty cubits wide. The four were of the same size.

23 On the inside, around each of the four courts was a row of masonry, with hearths made at the bottom of the rows all around.

24 And he said to me, These are the kitchens where those who minister at the temple shall boil the sacrifices of the people.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 46 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant liturgical calendar of Chapter 46 — Shabbat, new moon, appointed feasts — is the covenant time-structure of the restored community. The gate opens on Shabbat and new moon; the prince enters and worships; the people worship at the entrance. The covenant architectural and covenant temporal orders are integrated: the temple structure and the covenant calendar function together.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 46 in full. Verse 18 — the prince shall not take any of the inheritance of the people. The covenant land protection at the center of the covenant constitution: the prince may give from his own inheritance but may never seize from the people's covenant tribal portions. The Navot incident — the paradigmatic covenant land theft — is constitutionally prohibited in the restoration covenant.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 46. Verse 9 — he who enters by the north gate shall go out by the south gate, and he who enters by the south gate shall go out by the north gate. The covenant traffic pattern of the restored temple: worshipers flow through the sanctuary without retracing their steps. The covenant movement is forward, not circular — a covenant architectural expression of covenant progress.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 16-17 — if the prince makes a gift to his sons it is permanent inheritance; if to a servant, it reverts at the jubilee year. The covenant jubilee principle is embedded in the restoration covenant constitution: permanent alienation of covenant land is impossible outside the direct family line. The covenant economy of restoration is built on the Vayikra 25 jubilee framework, now applied even to the covenant prince's philanthropic gifts.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 47 The River From the Temple — Water Flowing East — Ankle Deep Then Knee Deep Then Waist Deep Then a River to Swim In — The Trees on the Banks — The Division of the Land

1 Then he brought me back to the door of the temple, and behold, water was issuing from below the threshold of the temple toward the east, for the temple faced east. The water was flowing down from below the south end of the threshold of the temple, south of the altar.

2 Then he brought me out by way of the north gate and led me around on the outside to the outer gate that faces toward the east, and behold, the water was trickling out on the south side.

3 Going on eastward with a measuring line in his hand, the man measured a thousand cubits, and then led me through the water, and it was ankle-deep.

4 Again he measured a thousand, and led me through the water, and it was knee-deep. Again he measured a thousand, and led me through the water, and it was waist-deep.

5 Again he measured a thousand, and it was a river that I could not pass through, for the water had risen. It was deep enough to swim in, a river that could not be passed through.

6 And he said to me, Son of man, have you seen this? Then he led me back to the bank of the river.

7 As I went back, I saw on the bank of the river very many trees on the one side and on the other.

8 And he said to me, This water flows toward the eastern region and goes down into the Aravah, and enters the sea. When the water flows into the sea, the water will become fresh.

9 And wherever the river goes, every living creature that swarms will live, and there will be very many fish. For this water goes there, that the waters of the sea may become fresh. So everything will live where the river goes.

10 Fishermen will stand beside the sea. From En-Gedi to En-Eglayim it will be a place for the spreading of nets. Its fish will be of very many kinds, like the fish of the Great Sea.

11 But its swamps and marshes will not become fresh. They are to be left for salt.

12 And on the banks, on both sides of the river, there will grow all kinds of trees for food. Their leaves will not wither, nor their fruit fail, but they will bear fresh fruit every month, because the water for them flows from the sanctuary. Their fruit will be for food, and their leaves for healing.

13 Thus says the Creator YHWH: This is the boundary by which you shall divide the land for inheritance among the twelve tribes of Yisra'EL. Yosef shall have two portions.

14 And you shall divide equally what I swore to give to your fathers. This land shall fall to you as your inheritance.

15 This shall be the boundary of the land: On the north side, from the Great Sea by way of Hethlon to Levo-Hamat, and on to Tzedad,

16 Berothah, Sivrayim — which lies on the border between Damascus and Hamat — as far as Hatzer-Hattikhon, which is on the border of Hawran.

17 So the boundary shall run from the sea to Hatzer-Enan, which is on the northern border of Damascus, with the border of Hamat to the north. This shall be the north side.

18 On the east side, the boundary shall run between Hawran and Damascus, along the Yarden between Gil'ad and the land of Yisra'EL. To the eastern sea and as far as Tamar. This shall be the east side.

19 On the south side, it shall run from Tamar as far as the waters of Merivat-Kadesh, from there along the Brook of Egypt to the Great Sea. This shall be the south side.

20 On the west side, the Great Sea shall be the boundary to a point opposite Levo-Hamat. This shall be the west side.

21 So you shall divide this land among you according to the tribes of Yisra'EL.

22 You shall allot it as an inheritance for yourselves and for the sojourners who reside among you and have had children among you. They shall be to you as native-born children of Yisra'EL. With you they shall be allotted an inheritance among the tribes of Yisra'EL.

23 In whatever tribe the sojourner resides, there you shall assign him his inheritance, declares the Creator YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 47 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The river flowing from the temple threshold — growing from trickle to swimming depth in four measured stages — is the covenant image of the covenant life that flows from YHWH's covenant presence. The covenant river transforms the Aravah — the deepest, saltiest, most desolate geographic feature of the covenant land — into a freshwater sea teeming with life.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 47 in full. Verse 22-23 — the sojourners who reside among you and have had children among you shall be to you as native-born. With you they shall be allotted an inheritance. The covenant universalism of the land allotment: resident sojourners — gerim — who have established families in the covenant land receive covenant inheritance alongside the native covenant community. The restored covenant community is not ethnically exclusive.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 47. Verse 12 — their fruit will be for food, and their leaves for healing. The covenant trees of the covenant river banks echo the covenant tree of life in Bereshit 2 and anticipate the covenant restoration of Bereshit's garden. The covenant river from the temple brings the covenant garden back: the desolation of exile is reversed by the covenant life-giving water from the covenant sanctuary.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3-5 — the fourfold deepening of the covenant river: ankle-deep, knee-deep, waist-deep, swimming-depth. The covenant progression is the covenant metaphor of increasing covenant immersion: the covenant life from the temple sanctuary begins as a trickle and becomes an uncrossable river. The covenant community's experience of covenant restoration moves from the barely perceptible to the overwhelmingly deep. The covenant river cannot be resisted — only swum in.

YEHEZKEL — CHAPTER 48 The Division of the Land Among the Twelve Tribes — The Holy Portion — The City — The Gates of the City — YHWH Is There

1 These are the names of the tribes: Beginning at the northern extreme, beside the way of Hethlon to Levo-Hamat, as far as Hatzer-Enan, which is on the northern border of Damascus over against Hamat, and extending from the east side to the west, Dan, one portion.

2 Adjoining the territory of Dan, from the east side to the west, Asher, one portion.

3 Adjoining the territory of Asher, from the east side to the west, Naftali, one portion.

4 Adjoining the territory of Naftali, from the east side to the west, Menasheh, one portion.

5 Adjoining the territory of Menasheh, from the east side to the west, Efrayim, one portion.

6 Adjoining the territory of Efrayim, from the east side to the west, Re'uven, one portion.

7 Adjoining the territory of Re'uven, from the east side to the west, Yehudah, one portion.

8 Adjoining the territory of Yehudah, from the east side to the west, shall be the portion that you shall set apart, twenty-five thousand cubits in breadth, and in length equal to one of the tribal portions, from the east side to the west, with the sanctuary in the midst of it.

9 The portion that you shall set apart for YHWH shall be twenty-five thousand cubits in length, and twenty thousand in breadth.

10 These shall be the allotments of the holy portion: the priests shall have an allotment measuring twenty-five thousand cubits on the north side, ten thousand cubits in breadth on the west side, ten thousand in breadth on the east side, and twenty-five thousand in length on the south side, with the sanctuary of YHWH in the midst of it.

11 This shall be for the consecrated priests, the sons of Tzadok, who kept my charge, who did not go astray when the people of Yisra'EL went astray, as the Levites did.

12 And it shall belong to them as a special portion from the holy portion of the land, a most holy place, adjoining the territory of the Levites.

13 And alongside the territory of the priests, the Levites shall have an allotment twenty-five thousand cubits in length and ten thousand in breadth. The whole length shall be twenty-five thousand cubits and the breadth twenty thousand.

14 They shall not sell or exchange any of it. They shall not alienate this choice portion of the land, for it is holy to YHWH.

15 The remainder, five thousand cubits in breadth and twenty-five thousand in length, shall be for common use for the city, for dwellings and for open country. The city shall be in the midst of it.

16 And these shall be its measurements: the north side four thousand five hundred cubits, the south side four thousand five hundred, the east side four thousand five hundred, and the west side four thousand five hundred.

17 And the city shall have open land: on the north two hundred and fifty cubits, on the south two hundred and fifty, on the east two hundred and fifty, and on the west two hundred and fifty.

18 The remainder of the length alongside the holy portion shall be ten thousand cubits to the east, and ten thousand to the west, and it shall be alongside the holy portion. Its produce shall be food for the workers of the city.

19 And the workers of the city, from all the tribes of Yisra'EL, shall till it.

20 The whole portion that you shall set apart shall be twenty-five thousand cubits square, that is, the holy portion together with the property of the city.

21 What remains on both sides of the holy portion and of the property of the city shall belong to the prince. Extending from the twenty-five thousand cubits of the holy portion to the east border, and westward from the twenty-five thousand cubits to the west border, parallel to the tribal portions, it shall belong to the prince. The holy portion with the sanctuary of the temple shall be in its midst.

22 Moreover, the property of the Levites and the property of the city shall be in the midst of that which belongs to the prince. The portion of the prince shall lie between the territory of Yehudah and the territory of Binyamin.

23 As for the rest of the tribes: from the east side to the west, Binyamin, one portion.

24 Adjoining the territory of Binyamin, from the east side to the west, Shim'on, one portion.

25 Adjoining the territory of Shim'on, from the east side to the west, Yissachar, one portion.

26 Adjoining the territory of Yissachar, from the east side to the west, Zevulun, one portion.

27 Adjoining the territory of Zevulun, from the east side to the west, Gad, one portion.

28 And adjoining the territory of Gad to the south, the boundary shall run from Tamar to the waters of Merivat-Kadesh, from there along the Brook of Egypt to the Great Sea.

29 This is the land that you shall allot as an inheritance among the tribes of Yisra'EL, and these are their portions, declares the Creator YHWH.

30 These shall be the exits of the city: On the north side, which is to be four thousand five hundred cubits by measure,

31 Three gates, the gate of Re'uven, the gate of Yehudah, and the gate of Levi, the gates of the city being named after the tribes of Yisra'EL.

32 On the east side, which is to be four thousand five hundred cubits, three gates: the gate of Yosef, the gate of Binyamin, and the gate of Dan.

33 On the south side, which is to be four thousand five hundred cubits by measure, three gates: the gate of Shim'on, the gate of Yissachar, and the gate of Zevulun.

34 On the west side, which is to be four thousand five hundred cubits, three gates: the gate of Gad, the gate of Asher, and the gate of Naftali.

35 The circumference of the city shall be eighteen thousand cubits. And the name of the city from that time on shall be: YHWH Is There.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS preserves Chapter 48 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 48 is the covenant conclusion of the entire book: the tribes receive their covenant portions in the restored land, arranged symmetrically around the central holy district with the temple at its center. The twelve-gated city with each gate bearing a tribal name is the covenant architectural declaration of the comprehensive inclusion of all twelve covenant tribes in the covenant restoration.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 48 in full. Verse 35 — the name of the city from that time on shall be: YHWH Is There. The final word of the entire book of Yehezkel. The book began with the kavod departing from the city — the covenant crisis of YHWH's absence. The book ends with the covenant name that declares YHWH's permanent presence in the restored city. The covenant trajectory from absence to presence is complete.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 48. The tribal allotments are arranged in seven portions north of the holy district — Dan, Asher, Naftali, Menasheh, Efrayim, Re'uven, Yehudah — and five portions south — Binyamin, Shim'on, Yissachar, Zevulun, Gad. The covenant symmetry is the covenant architectural expression of covenant completeness: all twelve tribes in their covenant places around the covenant center.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 35 — YHWH Shammah — YHWH Is There. The covenant name is the covenant answer to every question raised by the entire book. Why did Yerushalayim fall? Because YHWH departed — Chapters 10-11. What is the covenant hope of restoration? That YHWH will return — Chapter 43. What is the covenant name of the restored city that confirms the covenant restoration is complete? YHWH Is There. The covenant theology of the book is entirely contained in this final covenant name: presence, departure, return, permanent presence.

END OF PART SIX — CHAPTERS 40 THROUGH 48

Volume Nineteen — Yehezkel — Complete

𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

YHWH SHAMMAH — YHWH IS THERE

Yehezkel 48:35
